BLDC Motor Control

Note

License reminder: this project is distributed under the MIT License. See the repository LICENSE file. Disclaimer: this simulator is provided as-is for research, calibration, and educational use. Users assume all risks, including any direct or indirect damage, data loss, hardware damage, injury, or regulatory non-compliance arising from use or misuse.

src.core.motor_model

BLDC Motor Model

This module implements the mathematical model of a Brushless DC (BLDC) motor, including electrical and mechanical dynamics equations. The model accepts 3-phase voltages and produces motor variables (currents, EMF, torque, speed, position).

The electrical model is based on: - Phase voltage equation: v_phase = R*i_phase + L*di_phase/dt + e_back_phase - Back-EMF generation from rotor position

The mechanical model includes: - Newton’s second law: tau_electro - tau_load - f*omega = J*d(omega)/dt - Position integration: d(theta)/dt = omega

author:

BLDC Control Team

version:

0.8.0

Added in version 0.8.0: Initial implementation of BLDC motor model

Classes

BLDCMotor(parameters[, dt])

BLDC Motor Model

MotorParameters([model_type, emf_shape, ...])

Data class containing BLDC motor parameters.
class src.core.motor_model.MotorParameters(model_type='scalar', emf_shape='trapezoidal', nominal_voltage=48.0, phase_resistance=2.5, phase_inductance=0.005, back_emf_constant=0.1, torque_constant=0.1, rotor_inertia=0.0005, friction_coefficient=0.001, num_poles=8, poles_pairs=4, ld=None, lq=None, flux_weakening_id_coefficient=0.0, flux_weakening_min_ratio=0.2)[source]

Bases: object

Data class containing BLDC motor parameters.

Variables:

  • model_type – Type of motor model (‘scalar’ or ‘dq’)

  • nominal_voltage – Nominal DC voltage [V]

  • phase_resistance – Phase winding resistance [Ohms]

  • phase_inductance – Phase winding inductance [H]

  • back_emf_constant – Back-EMF constant [V*s/rad]

  • torque_constant – Electromagnetic torque constant [N*m/A]

  • rotor_inertia – Rotor moment of inertia [kg*m^2]

  • friction_coefficient – Viscous friction coefficient [N*m*s/rad]

  • num_poles – Number of magnetic poles

  • poles_pairs – Number of pole pairs (num_poles/2)

model_type: str = 'scalar'
emf_shape: str = 'trapezoidal'
nominal_voltage: float = 48.0
phase_resistance: float = 2.5
phase_inductance: float = 0.005
back_emf_constant: float = 0.1
torque_constant: float = 0.1
rotor_inertia: float = 0.0005
friction_coefficient: float = 0.001
num_poles: int = 8
poles_pairs: int = 4
ld: float | None = None
lq: float | None = None
flux_weakening_id_coefficient: float = 0.0
flux_weakening_min_ratio: float = 0.2
__init__(model_type='scalar', emf_shape='trapezoidal', nominal_voltage=48.0, phase_resistance=2.5, phase_inductance=0.005, back_emf_constant=0.1, torque_constant=0.1, rotor_inertia=0.0005, friction_coefficient=0.001, num_poles=8, poles_pairs=4, ld=None, lq=None, flux_weakening_id_coefficient=0.0, flux_weakening_min_ratio=0.2)
class src.core.motor_model.BLDCMotor(parameters, dt=0.0001)[source]

Bases: object

BLDC Motor Model

Implements a 3-phase BLDC motor model with electrical and mechanical dynamics. Uses numerical integration (4th-order Runge-Kutta) for state evolution.

The motor model tracks: - Three phase currents: i_a, i_b, i_c [A] - Rotor angular velocity: omega [rad/s] - Rotor angular position: theta [rad] - Phase voltages (input): v_a, v_b, v_c [V]

State Vector: [i_a, i_b, i_c, omega, theta]

Example:
>>> params = MotorParameters(
...     nominal_voltage=48.0,
...     phase_resistance=2.5,
...     phase_inductance=0.005,
...     back_emf_constant=0.1,
...     torque_constant=0.1,
...     rotor_inertia=0.0005,
...     friction_coefficient=0.001,
...     num_poles=8,
...     poles_pairs=4
... )
>>> motor = BLDCMotor(params, dt=0.0001)
>>> voltages = np.array([10.0, -5.0, -5.0])
>>> motor.step(voltages, load_torque=0.5)
__init__(parameters, dt=0.0001)[source]

Initialize the BLDC motor model.

Parameters:

  • parameters (MotorParameters) – Motor parameters (MotorParameters)

  • dt (float) – Simulation time step [seconds], defaults to 0.0001

Raises:

  • ValueError – If dt <= 0

  • ValueError – If phase_inductance <= 0

property currents: ndarray

Get phase currents [A].

Returns:

Array of [i_a, i_b, i_c]

Return type:

np.ndarray

property omega: float

Get rotor angular velocity [rad/s].

Returns:

Angular velocity

Return type:

float

property theta: float

Get rotor angular position [rad].

Returns:

Angular position (wrapped to [0, 2*pi])

Return type:

float

property speed_rpm: float

Get rotor speed in revolutions per minute [RPM].

Returns:

Speed in RPM

Return type:

float

property back_emf: ndarray

Get back-EMF for each phase [V].

Returns:

Array of [e_a, e_b, e_c]

Return type:

np.ndarray

property electromagnetic_torque: float

Get electromagnetic torque [N*m].

Returns:

Torque

Return type:

float

step(voltages, load_torque=0.0)[source]

Execute one simulation step using 4th-order Runge-Kutta integration.

Updates internal state: [i_a, i_b, i_c, omega, theta]

Runge-Kutta 4th Order (RK4):

\[k_1 = f(t_n, y_n)\]
\[k_2 = f(t_n + \frac{h}{2}, y_n + \frac{h}{2}k_1)\]
\[k_3 = f(t_n + \frac{h}{2}, y_n + \frac{h}{2}k_2)\]
\[k_4 = f(t_n + h, y_n + h*k_3)\]
\[y_{n+1} = y_n + \frac{h}{6}(k_1 + 2k_2 + 2k_3 + k_4)\]
Parameters:

  • voltages (ndarray) – Applied phase voltages [v_a, v_b, v_c] [V]

  • load_torque (float) – Load torque acting on motor shaft [N*m], defaults to 0.0

Return type:

None

Warning

Voltages must be 3-element array. Use SVM generator for modulated voltages.

reset(initial_speed=0.0)[source]

Reset motor to initial conditions.

Parameters:

initial_speed (float) – Initial rotor speed [rad/s], defaults to 0.0

Return type:

None

get_state_dict()[source]

Get all motor state variables as a dictionary.

Returns:

Dictionary containing all motor variables

Return type:

dict

Keys:

  • ‘currents_a’: Phase A current [A]

  • ‘currents_b’: Phase B current [A]

  • ‘currents_c’: Phase C current [A]

  • ‘omega’: Angular velocity [rad/s]

  • ‘theta’: Angular position [rad]

  • ‘speed_rpm’: Speed [RPM]

  • ‘back_emf_a’: Phase A back-EMF [V]

  • ‘back_emf_b’: Phase B back-EMF [V]

  • ‘back_emf_c’: Phase C back-EMF [V]

  • ‘torque’: Electromagnetic torque [N*m]
